meaning: n.一种粒状火成岩,钙长石
examples: Seams in the anorthosite series are only intermittently exposed.
HOUSTON - in the lab, the Moon rocks look nondescript - dark gray basalt, a whitish mineral called anorthosite and mixtures of the two with crystals thrown in.
The anorthosite underwent at least two more times of deformation than the norite, suggesting that the norite was emplaced after the consolidation and cooling of the anorthosite.
For the good of the country, our legislators decided that owners of anorthosite mines should be able to claim imaginary depreciation.
If you have an anorthosite mine and you are rich, or if your corporation has a mine, the penalty must be paid.
